summary: Jeff Atwood explores the history and cultural significance of the ping utility, created by Mike Muuss in 1983 at the U.S. Army Research Laboratory. He shares Muuss's account of coding ping in a single night to diagnose network issues, noting the creator's wistful reflection that had he known it would be his most famous accomplishment, he might have added more features. Atwood acknowledges that while ping is less useful on the modern internet due to filtering, it remains essential for local network diagnostics. The post culminates in sharing a legendary Amazon review that humorously interprets the children's book 'The Story About Ping' as a technical allegory for the ping utility.
key_points: Mike Muuss created ping in December 1983 in a single overnight coding session, including writing kernel support for raw ICMP sockets
Muuss expressed mild regret about not spending more time on what became his most famous creation
Ping's utility has diminished on the public internet due to widespread filtering, but remains valuable for local network troubleshooting
The famous satirical Amazon review brilliantly reinterprets a 1933 children's book as Unix networking documentation
Simple, focused tools that do one thing well can have lasting impact spanning decades
money_quotes: If I'd known then that it would be my most famous accomplishment in life, I might have worked on it another day or two and added some more options.
The code compiled just fine, but it didn't work – there was no kernel support for raw ICMP sockets! Incensed, I coded up the kernel support and had everything working well before sunrise.
Using deft allegory, the authors have provided an insightful and intuitive explanation of one of Unix's most venerable networking utilities.
The ping packet is described as a duck, who, with other packets (more ducks), spends a certain period of time on the host machine (the wise-eyed boat).
It's simple. It's utilitarian. And it does exactly what the sonar inspired name implies.

Who can read that passage on the Windows API ('Obscure, profound it was, and nebulous, So that by fixing on its depths my sight – Nothing whatever I discerned therein.'), without shaking their head with deep understanding.
key_insight: The most enduring software tools are often simple utilities created quickly to solve immediate problems, yet their creators rarely anticipate the lasting impact of their work.
